 {"id":"gt-atqr8","title":"Create session name helper functions in internal/session","description":"attached_args: Session name helper functions

Create helper functions for consistent session name construction.

## Files to modify
- internal/session/names.go (NEW)

## Implementation
Create new file with these functions:
```go
package session

import "fmt"

const Prefix = "gt-"

func MayorSessionName() string { return Prefix + "mayor" }
func DeaconSessionName() string { return Prefix + "deacon" }
func WitnessSessionName(rig string) string { return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s-witness", Prefix, rig) }
func RefinerySessionName(rig string) string { return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s-refinery", Prefix, rig) }
func CrewSessionName(rig, name string) string { return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s-crew-%s", Prefix, rig, name) }
func PolecatSessionName(rig, name string) string { return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s-%s", Prefix, rig, name) }
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New file internal/session/names.go created
- [ ] All 6 helper functions implemented
- [ ] Unit tests in internal/session/names_test.go
- [ ] go build ./... passes
- [ ] go test ./internal/session/... passes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:49:08.856968-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-28T16:12:31.8143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T16:12:31.8143-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-l9g","title":"Beads epic templates for batch work patterns","description":"Optional: Define templates for common batch work patterns.

## Concept

A template encodes a workflow pattern that can be instantiated as beads:

```yaml
# templates/batch-basic.yaml
name: basic-batch
description: Simple batch work pattern
phases:
  - name: startup
    issues:
      - title: "Verify workers ready"
  - name: working
    # Actual work issues added separately
  - name: cleanup
    issues:
      - title: "Merge all branches"
      - title: "Clean up workers"
      - title: "Report to Mayor"
```

## Usage

```bash
gt spawn --template basic-batch --epic gt-u1j --workers 3
```

Creates beads epic with template phases + actual work from gt-u1j children.

## Decision Point

Templates are OPTIONAL. The core design (beads as state, multi-wave orchestration) works without templates. Templates are sugar for common patterns.

Consider deferring to P3 or dropping entirely if beads epics with dependencies suffice.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" involved - templates just pre-populate epic/issue structure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:24.399235-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:08.868396-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zvte2","title":"Standardize agent bead naming: prefix-rig-role-name","description":"## The Problem

Agent bead IDs use INCONSISTENT naming patterns:

| Current (WRONG) | Should Be (prefix-rig-role-name) |
|-----------------|----------------------------------|
| gt-witness-gastown | gt-gastown-witness |
| gt-refinery-gastown | gt-gastown-refinery |
| gt-crew-gastown-max | gt-gastown-crew-max |
| gt-polecat-gastown-Toast | gt-gastown-polecat-Toast |

Town-level agents (no rig) are OK:
- gt-mayor ✓
- gt-deacon ✓

## The Convention

```
prefix-rig-role-name

Where:
- prefix: always 'gt'
- rig: rig name (absent for town-level agents)
- role: witness|refinery|crew|polecat|mayor|deacon
- name: agent name (absent for singleton roles like witness/refinery)
```

Examples:
- `gt-gastown-witness` (rig=gastown, role=witness, no name)
- `gt-gastown-crew-max` (rig=gastown, role=crew, name=max)
- `gt-beads-polecat-Toast` (rig=beads, role=polecat, name=Toast)
- `gt-mayor` (no rig, role=mayor, no name)

## Files to Update

### Agent bead ID generation
- internal/cmd/status.go (lines 316-335)
- internal/daemon/lifecycle.go (lines 546-579)
- internal/cmd/crew_add.go
- internal/polecat/manager.go
- internal/rig/manager.go
- internal/doctor/agent_beads_check.go
- internal/cmd/prime.go
- internal/session/names.go (session names vs bead IDs)

### Commands to verify
- gt status - displays agent beads
- gt doctor - creates missing agent beads
- gt rig add - creates witness/refinery beads
- gt crew add - creates crew beads
- gt sling - routes by agent ID
- gt mol status - looks up agent by ID

## Migration

1. Update all ID generation code
2. Create new agent beads with correct IDs
3. Migrate hook/role slots to new beads
4. Update any mail addresses
5. Delete old incorrectly-named beads

## Also from this session

Role bead slot cleanup was done - CreateAgentBead now calls bd slot set.
But legacy description parsing still exists in daemon/lifecycle.go.

## Reference
- ~/gt/docs/agent-as-bead.md
